web.title: Release notes for 5.0 | ArcGIS Maps SDK for JavaScript
web.description: Release notes for 5.0 (February 2026) ... Version 5.0 of the ArcGIS Maps SDK for JavaScript uses Calcite Design System, version 5.0.

_February 2026_

## Improved drawing experience [improved drawing experience permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#improved-drawing-experience)

The drawing system has been overhauled to enhance both new and existing 2D workflows, introducing true curve geometry support and a segment-focused drawing experience.

### Segment-based drawing [segment based drawing permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#segment-based-drawing)

Users can now switch between drawing tools (line, freehand, and new curve tools) while actively drawing geometry segments on a polyline or polygon. This improvement allows for finer-grained control when digitizing geometries. This is integrated natively into the [Sketch](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-sketch/) and [Editor](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-editor/) component user interfaces.

### Curve support [curve support permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#curve-support)

At this release, we added support for drawing, editing, querying, and snapping with true curve segments. A user will now be able to construct geometries containing curve segments when drawing. New curve tools include Bezier curve, Arc segment, and Endpoint arc segment tools, enabling the construction of Bezier and circular arcs.

Additionally, it is now possible to convert an existing straight-line segment to circular arcs by holding `Shift` and dragging the midpoint vertex manipulator of the straight-line segment.
The new curve drawing tools are integrated natively into the [Sketch](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-sketch/) and [Editor](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-editor/) components.

## Editor snapping and true curve support [editor snapping and true curve support permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#editor-snapping-and-true-curve-support)

### Update and create workflows with true curves [update and create workflows with true curves permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#update-and-create-workflows-with-true-curves)

[`Editor`](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-editor/) now natively supports adding and updating geometries with true curves. For editing workflows, support is dependent on the feature service capabilities. The drawing toolbar will display the segment tools while drawing a polyline or polygon, including the new curve tools.

- **Update**: If the feature service supports true curves, [Editor](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-editor/) fetches and allows modification of curve geometries. If the service does not support updating true curves, then geometry updates will be turned off for the true curve geometries.

- **Create**: Regardless of whether the feature service supports true curves or not, [Editor](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-editor/) will provide the curve drawing tools to construct curve geometries. If the service doesn’t support the drawn curve type, segments are densified upon submission, with a warning provided to the user.


### Snapping with true curves [snapping with true curves permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#snapping-with-true-curves)

The snapping engine now supports true curves, allowing snapping to any point along a curve segment. Snapping behavior depends on feature service support; unsupported curve types revert to densified geometry snapping.

## FeatureTable enhancements [featuretable enhancements permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#featuretable-enhancements)

### Contingent Attribute Value Constraints [contingent attribute value constraints permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#contingent-attribute-value-constraints)

The [FeatureTable](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-feature-table/) component and [widget](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/widgets/FeatureTable/) now respect contingent attribute value constraints during cell editing, with no additional configuration required. When contingent values are configured in the data, the FeatureTable automatically presents the recommended values while editing. Automatic saving is disabled so that invalid entries are flagged in the UI, giving users a chance to correct them before saving.

### Disable auto-save behavior [disable auto save behavior permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#disable-auto-save-behavior)

It is now possible to disable automatic saving while editing in the [FeatureTable](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-feature-table/) component and [widget](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/widgets/FeatureTable/). By default, any updates made to individual cell values are saved without needing any manual interaction to save. This behavior is disabled by default for data that contains contingent attribute values. Applications can be configured to disable automatic saving regardless of the dataset being edited. This is handled by setting the [autoSaveDisabled](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-feature-table/#autoSaveDisabled) to `true`. The FeatureTable will display a UI to proceed with saving edits.

### Improved attachment viewing experience [improved attachment viewing experience permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#improved-attachment-viewing-experience)

The default attachment viewing experience in the FeatureTable [component](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-feature-table/) and [widget](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/widgets/FeatureTable/) has been enhanced to provide richer file details, including name, type, size, and keywords. Image attachments can now be viewed directly within the table and expanded to full-page view without opening a new browser tab. This improvement streamlines review and editing workflows while minimizing context switching.

![FeatureTable attachment experience](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/_astro/50-featuretable-attachment-ui-1.CaLmEtbV_Z1ER9Es.webp)

## Syncing selection via SelectionManager (beta) [syncing selection via selectionmanager beta permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#syncing-selection-via-selectionmanager-beta)

The [SelectionManager](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/views/SelectionManager/) (currently in [beta](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/faq/#what-does-the-beta-tag-mean)), manages feature selection across layers and tables within a [MapView](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/views/MapView/) or [SceneView](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/views/SceneView/) to help manage feature selection. It can be configured to listen for edits on selectable layers and automatically update selections in response, providing a centralized way to manage selection state within a view.

To support this workflow, new properties were added to the [Editor](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-editor/#syncViewSelection) and [FeatureTable](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-feature-table/#syncViewSelection) components and widgets. The `syncViewSelection` property allows a component to participate in the view’s shared selection state by syncing with the view’s [selectionManager](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/views/View/#selectionManager). When `syncViewSelection` is set to true, the component responds to selection changes managed by the view. This enables selections to remain consistent across different parts of an application that rely on the view’s selection state, without requiring direct coordination between individual components. The video below demonstrates this.

## Light-emitting symbols and glow [light emitting symbols and glow permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#light-emitting-symbols-and-glow)

3D scenes now support light-emitting visualization through [emissive material properties](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/symbols/support/Symbol3DEmissive/) and a global [Glow](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/webscene/Glow/) effect.

Emissive materials make symbols produce light, enabling realistic nighttime scenes as well as visual emphasis and improved legibility. The emissive `strength` of individual symbols controls how much light they emit and can be used as an additional visual aspect to communicate information.

Any volumetric symbol can be turned into a light-emitting element by using `source: "color"` — well-suited for highlighting selected features, routes, or categories that should stand out in a scene. For realistic nighttime rendering, set `source: "emissive"` to display emissive information authored directly into 3D models (such as illuminated signage or windows) so they render naturally.

An optional global `glow` effect can be enabled to apply a more stylized look across the scene. Without glow, emissive elements display with a bright, natural appearance.

See the [Visualizations with light-emitting symbols](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/sample-code/light-emitting-symbols/index.html) sample to learn how to use emissive materials and glow to highlight features and build nighttime scenes.

## Gaussian splat layer [gaussian splat layer permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#gaussian-splat-layer)

We are introducing the [GaussianSplatLayer](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/layers/GaussianSplatLayer/), a new 3D layer type that provides photorealistic visualization of complex built and natural environments in a [3D Scene](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-scene/). It delivers high-detail rendering for thin and intricate structures such as powerlines, railings, antennas, and dense vegetation, along with smooth transparency for materials like glass and other semi-translucent surfaces. These enhancements support advanced workflows like inspection, vegetation analysis, and high-fidelity presentation workflows.

[GaussianSplatLayer](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/layers/GaussianSplatLayer/) integrates seamlessly with existing 3D and 2D content, enhancing overall scene realism. A multi-resolution level-of-detail system maintains smooth navigation by drawing higher detail only where it matters most. For data preparation, refer to the [GaussianSplatLayer documentation](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/layers/GaussianSplatLayer/). The layer also supports the [ESRI\_CRS vendor extension for 3D Tiles](https://github.com/Esri/3D-tiles-layer/blob/main/ESRI_CRS/Esri_crs_extension.md), ensuring correct visualization of projected datasets in both local and global scenes.

A new [sample](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/sample-code/layers-gaussiansplatlayer/) is available — jump in and experience how simple it is to add and visualize a Gaussian splat layer in a scene.

## AI components (beta) [ai components beta permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#ai-components-beta)

The new [AI components (beta)](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/ai-components/) package contains a set of components that enables developers to build agentic mapping web applications. It includes the [Assistant](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/ai-components/components/arcgis-assistant/) component, which provides a chat interface that allows users to interact with a web map and its data using natural language.

This package also includes the following agents that can be registered with the Assistant component:

- [Navigation Agent](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/ai-components/components/arcgis-assistant-navigation-agent/)
- [Data Exploration Agent](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/ai-components/components/arcgis-assistant-data-exploration-agent/)
- [Help Agent](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/ai-components/components/arcgis-assistant-help-agent/)

Read more about how to build agentic web applications in the [AI components](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/agentic-apps/ai-introduction/) guide.

### New map components [new map components permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#new-map-components)

The following map components were added at this release:

### Refactoring web components [refactoring web components permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#refactoring-web-components)

The following components in `map-components` have been updated internally so they no longer wrap widget code in their implementation for this release:

- [Area Measurement 2D](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-area-measurement-2d/)
- [Building Explorer](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-building-explorer/)

- [Shadow Cast](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-shadow-cast/)

By making components consistent across the SDK, their behavior will also more closely mimic what is expected from a standard web component.
We will continue to work on the remaining components in `@arcgis/map-components` that are currently wrapping widget code in their implementation.
See [Esri’s move to web components](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/components-transition-plan/) for more information about the widget transition period and future.

### Adjust component size [adjust component size permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#adjust-component-size)

## New analysis-based components [new analysis based components permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#new-analysis-based-components)

[Elevation Profile](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-elevation-profile/), and
[Shadow Cast](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-shadow-cast/) components have been refactored to utilize the
[ElevationProfileAnalysis](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/analysis/ElevationProfileAnalysis/) and the
[ShadowCastAnalysis](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/analysis/ShadowCastAnalysis/). Similarly,
[Volume Measurement](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-volume-measurement/) (beta) was created as a new component on top of
[VolumeMeasurementAnalysis](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/analysis/VolumeMeasurementAnalysis/).
This means the underlying logic of all these components is managed by an analysis object, while the component handles
the UI and user interactions.
When you connect an analysis-based component to a [Map](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-map/) or
[Scene](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-scene/), it automatically creates an analysis object and adds it to the
`view.analyses` collection. The analysis is then automatically removed when the component is destroyed.

## Utility Network Trace Analysis component [utility network trace analysis component permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#utility-network-trace-analysis-component)

[Utility Network Trace Analysis component](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-utility-network-trace-analysis/) is now available. The [Utility Network Trace Analysis component](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-utility-network-trace-analysis/) supersedes the [Utility Network Trace component](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-utility-network-trace/) and is now the preferred approach for running Utility Network traces. The [Utility Network Trace component](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-utility-network-trace/) is deprecated and planned for removal in a future major release.

## Volume Measurement component (beta) [volume measurement component beta permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#volume-measurement-component-beta)

The new [Volume Measurement component](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-volume-measurement/) is now available in [beta](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/faq/#what-does-the-beta-tag-mean). It provides a declarative way to add volume measurement capabilities to web scenes using _Cut & Fill_ and _Stockpile_ modes. To use the Volume Measurement component, add it to the Scene component:

```
<arcgis-scene item-id="45f0389d12c44784891ac11342b94dd5">

  <arcgis-volume-measurement slot="top-right"></arcgis-volume-measurement>

</arcgis-scene>
```

## Oriented Imagery [oriented imagery permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#oriented-imagery)

- The Oriented Imagery Viewer [component](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-oriented-imagery-viewer/) (and [widget](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/widgets/OrientedImageryViewer/)) interface has been redesigned to deliver a more streamlined and intuitive user experience.
Key tools with overlapping or related functionality are now grouped under consolidated parent toggles, reducing visual clutter and providing a cleaner, more organized layout.
- A new Search Tools group has been introduced to the component, providing three options for accessing imagery from an oriented imagery layer:
  - Explore Images: Performs a best image search based on the user-selected map location.
  - Explore Images 3D: Extends the explore images workflow by incorporating height-based filtering using the feature’s Z values.
  - Display Images: Allows users to select an oriented imagery feature directly on the map or scene to view its associated image.
- Image caching can now be enabled during directional and sequential navigation, improving overall viewer performance and reducing image load times.
- Time Info filtering is now supported when querying for images, enabling retrieval based on temporal constraints for more precise and context-aware results.

## Attribution updates [attribution updates permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#attribution-updates)

Attribution displays pipe (`|`) separators between items instead of commas and was enhanced to avoid duplication of attribution items.

## Popup enhancements [popup enhancements permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#popup-enhancements)

### Default Popup component [default popup component permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#default-popup-component)

The [Popup component](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-popup/) ( [beta](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/faq/#what-does-the-beta-tag-mean)) can now be used as the default popup for the [Map](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-map/#popupComponentEnabled), [Scene](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-scene/#popupComponentEnabled), and [Link Chart](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-link-chart/#popupComponentEnabled) components by setting the `popup-component-enabled` attribute. This enables the popup component to open automatically (instead of the widget) when features are selected in the view component.

```
<arcgis-map item-id="WEBMAP-ID" popup-component-enabled></arcgis-map>
```

When the popup component is enabled, the new `popupElement` property on the [Map](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-map/#popupElement), [Scene](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-scene/#popupElement), and [Link Chart](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-link-chart/#popupElement) components can be used to reference the default popup component to set properties such as docking options.

```
const viewElement = document.querySelector("arcgis-map");

await viewElement.viewOnReady();

viewElement.popupElement.dockEnabled = true;

viewElement.popupElement.dockOptions = {

    buttonEnabled: false,

    breakpoint: false,

    position: "top-right",

};
```

### AttachmentsContent refreshed design [attachmentscontent refreshed design permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#attachmentscontent-refreshed-design)

When viewing attachments with [AttachmentsContent](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/popup/content/AttachmentsContent/) in the [Popup](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-popup/) component, a refreshed design has been applied to improve usability and accessibility by updating it to use [Calcite Design System](https://developers.arcgis.com/calcite-design-system/).

### Edit waypoints within Directions component [edit waypoints within directions component permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#edit-waypoints-within-directions-component)

The [Directions component](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-directions/) now allows users to fine-tune routes with waypoints in a familiar “drag-and-drop” experience. Waypoints are locations that routes must travel through but do not need to stop at.
Selecting the `Edit route` button now allows you to add, move, and remove waypoints. To add a new waypoint, first hover the cursor over the route and select the desired point. Then select and drag the point to move and place the waypoint.

![drag-and-drop](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/_astro/dragndrop.B7sCos9x_Z11gdRB.webp)

## Printing UI updates and dynamic legend items [printing ui updates and dynamic legend items permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#printing-ui-updates-and-dynamic-legend-items)

We enhanced printing to deliver a better user experience with several new capabilities. First, the [Print](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-print/) component now allows users to choose scalebar units directly in the UI.
We also added a configurable property that automatically populates the Author field when the user is signed in to a portal.
Finally, we added support for [Dynamic legend items](https://pro.arcgis.com/en/pro-app/latest/help/layouts/work-with-legend-items.htm#ESRI_SECTION1_2A6847768842437497B5F1AB7B74F528), which requires a minimum of an 11.4 print service. When enabled, only currently visible features appear in the printed legend. When disabled, all features appear in the printed legend. Because ArcGIS print services have dynamic‑legend enabled by default for all layout templates, this option lets users turn it off for templates that support it, which will print all features in the legend, whether visible or not. See the image below for an example.

| Dynamic legend enabled | Dynamic legend disabled |
| --- | --- |
| ![dynamic-enable](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/_astro/print-dynamic-enable.5Iilq2Jc_23DOn7.webp) | ![dynamic-disable](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/_astro/print-dynamic-disable.CL8uu3TB_Z1MXv43.webp) |

## BuildingFilter with BuildingSceneLayer [buildingfilter with buildingscenelayer permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#buildingfilter-with-buildingscenelayer)

As of this release, the [Building Explorer component](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-building-explorer/) no longer wraps the [Building Explorer widget](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/widgets/BuildingExplorer/). As a result, the associated [BuildingExplorerViewModel](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/widgets/BuildingExplorer/BuildingExplorerViewModel/), along with its subclasses [BuildingLevel](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/widgets/BuildingExplorer/BuildingLevel/) and [BuildingPhase](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/widgets/BuildingExplorer/BuildingPhase/), have been deprecated. The component’s public API has been enhanced with two new events, `arcgisBuildingLevelChange` and `arcgisBuildingPhaseChange`, which are emitted when the selected building level or construction phase changes.

Filtering features is an essential capability in BIM-related workflows. The [BuildingSceneLayer](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/layers/BuildingSceneLayer/) supports attribute-based filtering through the [BuildingFilter](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/layers/support/BuildingFilter/) class, which defines the expressions and visual modes that are added to the [BuildingSceneLayer#filters](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/layers/BuildingSceneLayer/#filters) to show or hide specific features. Filtering is based on _modelNames_ that are common to all BuildingSceneLayer. To filter a layer by level or construction phase, use `bldgLevel`, `createdPhase`, and `demolishedPhase` as the corresponding _modelNames_ in [#filterExpression](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/layers/support/BuildingFilterBlock/#filterExpression).

Explore this new [sample](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/sample-code/building-scene-layer-building-filter/) to see `BuildingFilter` in use.

## Charts components [charts components permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#charts-components)

### Class break support for heat charts [class break support for heat charts permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#class-break-support-for-heat-charts)

Class breaks can now be defined for heat charts to visualize data in distinct ranges. Use [`heatRulesType`](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/charts-components/model/heat-chart-model/heat-chart-model/#heatRulesType) from the heat chart model to switch between `renderer` and `gradient` legend types.

- When using class breaks, an extra null value marker is added to represent the empty cells.
- Customization can be done via the [`chartRenderer`](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/charts-components/model/heat-chart-model/heat-chart-model/#chartRenderer) property from the heat chart model.

## Common components [common components permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#common-components)

The new [Common components](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/) package contains a set of shared UI components used by some of the higher-level components in the JavaScript Maps SDK. It includes the [Slider](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-slider/), [Histogram](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-histogram/), and [Ticks](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/components/arcgis-ticks/) components.

[![ArcGIS Maps SDK common components](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/_astro/common-components.zh9OJY84_23AfGf.webp)](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/)

## CDN specific changes [cdn specific changes permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#cdn-specific-changes)

### Single script tag for CDN users [single script tag for cdn users permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#single-script-tag-for-cdn-users)

As of 5.0, the main CDN entry point at [https://js.arcgis.com/5.0/](https://js.arcgis.com/5.0/) is module-based and includes [core](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/), [map-components](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/), [charts-components](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/charts-components/), [coding-components](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/coding-components/), [ai-components](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/ai-components/), [common-components](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/common-components/), and [calcite-components](https://developers.arcgis.com/calcite-design-system/).

Thus, only a _single_ script tag is needed in CDN-based apps.

```
    <!-- Load the ArcGIS Maps SDK for JavaScript from CDN -->

    <script type="module" src="https://js.arcgis.com/5.0/"></script>
```

Prior to 5.0, you might have used multiple script tags like:

```
    <!-- Load Calcite components from CDN -->

    <script type="module" src="https://js.arcgis.com/calcite-components/3.3.3/calcite.esm.js"></script>

    <!-- Load the ArcGIS Maps SDK for JavaScript from CDN -->

    <script src="https://js.arcgis.com/4.34/"></script>

    <!-- Load Map components from CDN -->

    <script type="module" src="https://js.arcgis.com/4.34/map-components/"></script>
```

If you still want to use the old behavior to access _only_ the core API, use [https://js.arcgis.com/5.0/core.js](https://js.arcgis.com/5.0/core.js).

### Patch versions on CDN [patch versions on cdn permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#patch-versions-on-cdn)

As of 5.0, we provide CDN versions for specific patches in MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH format, for example [https://js.arcgis.com/5.0.0/](https://js.arcgis.com/5.0.0/). This URL will also be used as the default assetsPath for [⁠CDN-hosted assets](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/working-with-assets/) when working with npm and local builds.
The MAJOR.MINOR syntax for 5+ is an alias to the latest patch.
Use this syntax if you always want the latest CDN version, even though it might impact your application in unintended ways.

### TypeScript import changes [typescript import changes permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#typescript-import-changes)

The `__esri` namespace is no longer supported for importing types and will be removed at 6.0. Use individual ESM imports to directly import types from their respective module files instead.

See example below:

```
import type Geometry from "@arcgis/core/geometry/Geometry";

import type FeatureLayer from "@arcgis/core/layers/FeatureLayer";

let geometry: __esri.Geometry;

const layer = arcgisMapView.map?.allLayers.find((l) => l.id === layerId) as __esri.FeatureLayer;

let geometry: Geometry;

const layer = arcgisMapView.map?.allLayers.find((l) => l.id === layerId) as FeatureLayer;
```

#### Codemod to refactor types [codemod to refactor types permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#codemod-to-refactor-types)

A codemod has been provided that automatically refactors existing `__esri` usages to the recommended ESM imports. Please refer to the [documentation](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@arcgis/codemod).

## Auto-reposition polygon labels [auto reposition polygon labels permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#auto-reposition-polygon-labels)

We enhanced [polygon labeling](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/layers/support/LabelClass/#labelPlacement) by enhancing how labels are automatically repositioned when a polygon’s centroid is partially or fully out of view.
This results in more labels appearing fully visible, and even partial labels can now be placed in areas where labels previously could not be shown. In the image below, compare the county labels when zoomed-in close in Alaska. Most of the county areas are outside the view of the map, but now more labels can display.

| 4.34 | 5.0 |
| --- | --- |
| ![ak-counties-434](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/_astro/ak-counties-434.D1LddCFi_Z11nynI.webp) | ![ak-counties-50](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/_astro/ak-counties-50.Czas1vF8_2b7eOV.webp) |

## Improved Transparent Ground Rendering [improved transparent ground rendering permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#improved-transparent-ground-rendering)

We improved the rendering of transparent terrain for the Google Photorealistic 3D Basemap and the new label and icon
visibility modes. All transparent data, including terrain, is now rendered with the same order-independent
transparency algorithm. This algorithm produces a close approximation of the physically correct visualization.

Icons and labels behind transparent geometries now behave in the same way, no matter if they are behind terrain or
other data. For example, in mountainous areas it becomes easier to understand the spatial relationship of hills,
valleys, and 3D data on the ground.

The Google integrated mesh ground layer can also be visualized with partial opacity, and is also drawn with
order-independent transparency.

## New ShadowCastAnalysis [new shadowcastanalysis permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#new-shadowcastanalysis)

Shadow cast is now available as an [analysis object](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/analysis/ShadowCastAnalysis/), replacing the deprecated programmatic `ShadowCastViewModel` pattern and aligning with the SDK’s [analysis framework](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/analysis/Analysis/).

The analysis supports three visualization modes, as well as measuring shadow duration at the cursor location via [view.whenAnalysisView()](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/views/SceneView/#whenLayerView) and [getDurationAtScreen()](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/views/3d/analysis/ShadowCastAnalysisView3D/#getDurationAtScreen). You can use the analysis and build custom panels, workflows, controls, and integrations while following the same analysis lifecycle as other analysis types.

Explore it in the [Shadow Cast analysis object](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/sample-code/analysis-shadow-cast/) sample.

#### Migration notes [migration notes permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#migration-notes)

- The widget’s `visualizationType` maps to `mode`:

  - `threshold` → `min-duration`
  - `duration` → `total-duration`
  - `discrete` → `discrete`
- Mode-specific settings are now grouped under:
  - `minDurationOptions`, `totalDurationOptions`, and `discreteOptions`
- The corresponding [Shadow Cast](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-shadow-cast/) component provides a user interface to the analysis.

## Improved occlusion handling for 3D labels and icons [improved occlusion handling for 3d labels and icons permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#improved-occlusion-handling-for-3d-labels-and-icons)

This release introduces enhanced control over the visibility of labels and icons in 3D scenes with the new [occludedVisibility](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/symbols/support/OccludedVisibility/) property. Previously, labels and icons would disappear when hidden by buildings or terrain, causing users to lose context during navigation. Labels and icons now remain recognizable even when partially or fully occluded, providing better context, orientation, and spatial awareness.

When using either the [adaptive](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/symbols/support/OccludedVisibility/#mode) or [visible](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/symbols/support/OccludedVisibility/#mode) mode, occluded parts are rendered semi-opaque during navigation to provide depth cues, while labels become fully opaque when the view is idle for optimal readability. The new [occludedVisibility](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/symbols/support/OccludedVisibility/) property is available for both [IconSymbol3DLayer](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/symbols/IconSymbol3DLayer/#occludedVisibility) and [TextSymbol3DLayer](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/symbols/TextSymbol3DLayer/#occludedVisibility), supporting three modes:

- [Adaptive](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/symbols/support/OccludedVisibility/#mode) (default): Labels and icons that are behind other objects can still be rendered depending on how far behind an object they are. This automatically provides a good balance between depth cues, readability, and reduced distractions while navigating the scene. This mode is used in the 3D Basemaps.
- [Visible](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/symbols/support/OccludedVisibility/#mode): Labels and icons behind other objects are always rendered.
- [Hidden](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/symbols/support/OccludedVisibility/#mode): Labels and icons behind other objects are not rendered. This matches behavior of previous versions of the SDK.

Try out the new occlusion handling in the [Basemap Gallery sample](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/sample-code/basemap-gallery/), featuring New York with the Topographic 3D Basemap, and experience adaptive labels and icons as you navigate the city. Additionally, explore the extended [Point styles for cities sample](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/sample-code/visualization-point-styles/), now showcasing all three `occludedVisibility` modes, to see how each setting affects label and icon visibility in the scene.

## VolumeMeasurementAnalysis enhancements [volumemeasurementanalysis enhancements permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#volumemeasurementanalysis-enhancements)

[VolumeMeasurementAnalysis](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/analysis/VolumeMeasurementAnalysis/) was introduced as
[beta](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/faq/#what-does-the-beta-tag-mean) in 4.34,
allowing you to interactively measure and visualize ground surface volumes in 3D web scenes using _Cut & Fill_ and _Stockpile_ measurements.

With this release, we improved the consistency of the measurement experience and updated the API.

#### Notable improvements [notable improvements permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#notable-improvements)

- Improved the heuristics for accuracy estimation
- Added supporting area and perimeter measurements to the input polygon
- Added support for self-intersecting polygons
- Improved label placement

Explore it in the [Volume measurement analysis object sample](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/sample-code/analysis-volume-measurement/).

## ElevationProfileAnalysis out of beta [elevationprofileanalysis out of beta permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#elevationprofileanalysis-out-of-beta)

[ElevationProfileAnalysis](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/analysis/ElevationProfileAnalysis/) supports creating custom elevation profile workflows and was introduced as beta in version 4.34. It enables generating [profiles](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/analysis/ElevationProfileAnalysis/#profiles) from a drawn line or from an existing feature in the view using the new [setGeometryFromFeature()](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/analysis/ElevationProfileAnalysis/#setGeometryFromFeature) method. As of this release, the API is considered complete and stable.

Explore it in the [Elevation Profile Analysis sample](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/sample-code/analysis-elevation-profile/).

## Google Photorealistic 3D basemaps (beta) [google photorealistic 3d basemaps beta permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#google-photorealistic-3d-basemaps-beta)

The new Google Photorealistic 3D basemaps provide a three-dimensional mesh textured with high-resolution imagery. See
[3D Basemaps](https://doc.arcgis.com/en/arcgis-online/create-maps/choose-scene-basemap.htm#ESRI_SECTION1_5C993D2C5C314A5FAFAEDC01DE18B230)
for more information. In support of the new Photorealistic 3D basemaps, various related enhancements and fixes have
been made to the SDK, including performance improvements for 3D Tiles integrated meshes and improved rendering of
transparent ground.

![Google Photorealistic 3D Basemap](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/_astro/50-google-photorealistic-3d-basemap.CZk5gkVo_Z2w8dXk.webp)

## FlowRenderer - continuous flow in 2D [flowrenderer   continuous flow in 2d permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#flowrenderer---continuous-flow-in-2d)

The [FlowRenderer](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/renderers/FlowRenderer/) now supports continuous flow in 2D, resulting in a smoother experience when zooming, panning, or changing the time extent.

## Graphic’s origin [graphics origin permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#graphics-origin)

The [Graphic.origin](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/Graphic/#origin) property is now populated when working with various layer types, providing contextual information about where a graphic originates from. For example, graphic’s `origin` is populated for graphics returned from [MapView.hitTest](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/views/MapView/#hitTest) or `queryFeatures()` on a layer or layer view instance. Use the `Graphic.origin` property in place of the removed `Graphic.layer` property.

## Feature reduction field formatting [feature reduction field formatting permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#feature-reduction-field-formatting)

You can now use [field configurations](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/layers/support/FieldConfiguration/#FieldConfigurationProperties) with both [FeatureReductionBinning](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/layers/support/FeatureReductionBinning/#fieldConfigurations) and [FeatureReductionCluster](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/layers/support/FeatureReductionCluster/#fieldConfigurations) on service-backed [FeatureLayers](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/layers/FeatureLayer/#FeatureLayer). Incremental support for [field configurations](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/layers/support/FieldConfiguration/#FieldConfigurationProperties) will be introduced in upcoming releases, enhancing areas of the SDK that involve working with field information.

## Basemap sessions [basemap sessions permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#basemap-sessions)

Added a new [basemap session API](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/config/#Config-sessions) to support the [session usage model](https://developers.arcgis.com/documentation/mapping-and-location-services/mapping/basemaps/basemap-usage-styles/#session-usage-model) for basemap styles, providing more control over how basemap requests are authenticated in applications. This is only supported for Location Platform.

```
import config from "@arcgis/core/config";

config.apiKey = "YOUR_API_KEY";

config.sessions = {

  basemap: {

    enabled: true,

    styleFamily: "arcgis", // only access styles in the "arcgis" family

    autoRefresh: true, // automatically generate new token at end of duration

    duration: 3600, // session lasts 1 hour

  },

};
```

## The sql utility functions [the sql utility functions permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#the-sql-utility-functions)

Added [sqlBooleanLiteral](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/core/sql/#sqlBooleanLiteral), [sqlNumericLiteral](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/core/sql/#sqlNumericLiteral), [sqlStringLiteral](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/core/sql/#sqlStringLiteral), [sqlDateLiteral](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/core/sql/#sqlDateLiteral), [sqlTimeLiteral](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/core/sql/#sqlTimeLiteral), and [sqlTimestampLiteral](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/core/sql/#sqlTimestampLiteral) for safely creating SQL expressions from JavaScript values.
Typed values are converted as needed; untyped timestamps are passed through. For unknown types, use `sqlLiteral`.

## Documentation improvements [documentation improvements permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#documentation-improvements)

### Mapping application layouts [mapping application layouts permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#mapping-application-layouts)

A new guide page [Mapping application layouts](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/creating-app-layouts/) introduces recommended patterns for structuring web mapping applications. The guide covers layout responsibilities and best practices for building scalable applications using the Calcite Design System and JavaScript Maps SDK.
Three new layout-focused samples are now available under [layout applications](https://github.com/Esri/jsapi-resources/tree/main/layouts/).

### Get started with @arcgis/create [get started with arcgiscreate permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#get-started-with-arcgiscreate)

The [Get started](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/get-started/) guide has been updated with guidance on creating an app with npm using the [@arcgis/create](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@arcgis/create) CLI tool and starter templates from [Esri/jsapi-resources](https://github.com/Esri/jsapi-resources/tree/main). Now, you can create a ready-to-use application with one simple command:

```
npx @arcgis/create -n my-arcgis-app -t vite
```

For the full walkthrough and more template options, see [Create an app with npm](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/get-started/#create-an-app-with-npm) and [Use @arcgis/create](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/get-started/#use-the-sdks-create-cli). Template sources are in [jsapi-resources/templates](https://github.com/Esri/jsapi-resources/tree/main/templates/).

### Sample updates [sample updates permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#sample-updates)

The majority of the samples have been updated to use components to reflect the recommended usage patterns and [best practices](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/programming-patterns/). The [samples](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/sample-code/) have been refreshed with a new search experience and UI design, allowing you to view the description, sample code, and live sample within the same page. This update also provides a better experience for users on mobile devices.

### Improved types [improved types permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#improved-types)

With the [TypeScript import changes](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/#typescript-import-changes), the TypeScript definitions now include type parameters, providing stronger type checking and an improved developer experience. This update provides more detailed and accurate information about types, parameters, and return values. Due to the improved types, some existing code may require adjustments to comply with the updated type definitions. See the added types in the [SDK changes](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/#map-components-changes) for more details.

### Documentation for previous versions [documentation for previous versions permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#documentation-for-previous-versions)

Starting with version 4.34, we plan to make previous versions of the documentation easily available for supported versions of the SDK. These will be hosted on our website, allowing you to access the specific documentation you need for your project instead of having to download the SDK locally.

## Core API changes [core api changes permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#core-api-changes)

- Importing JS Maps SDK modules using `require()` is deprecated since version 5.0 and may be removed at version 6.0. [`$arcgis.import()`](https://www.esri.com/arcgis-blog/products/js-api-arcgis/developers/future-proof-your-arcgis-javascript-app) is the preferred way to import modules in your HTML applications
- The `__esri` namespace is no longer supported for importing types and will be removed at 6.0. Use individual ESM imports to directly import types from their respective module files instead. See [TypeScript import changes](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/#typescript-import-changes) for more information.

- [Basemap](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/Basemap/)

  - UniqueValueRenderer - **removed** type declaration - deprecated since version 4.32. Import [UniqueValueRenderer](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/renderers/UniqueValueRenderer/) directly instead.
  - VectorFieldRenderer - **removed** type declaration - deprecated since version 4.32. Import [VectorFieldRenderer](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/renderers/VectorFieldRenderer/) directly instead.

## Breaking changes [breaking changes permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#breaking-changes)

### Core API breaking changes [core api breaking changes permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#core-api-breaking-changes)

- Setting locale with `dojoConfig.locale` is no longer supported. See the [Localization](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/localization/) guide for more information on how to set the locale in your JavaScript Maps SDK applications.
- The following named `easing` presets on [GoToOptions3D](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/views/SceneView/#GoToOptions3D) presets were deprecated at 4.33 and have now been removed: `in-cubic`, `out-cubic`, `in-out-cubic`, `in-expo`, `out-expo`, `in-out-expo`, and `in-out-coast-quad`. Please use a supported [EasingName](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/views/animation/types/#EasingName) instead.
- The [IPSInfo.SmoothingProperties](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/webdoc/ips/SmoothingProperties/#enabled) are no longer enabled by default. To enable smoothing, set the `enabled` property to `true`.
- Attribution is no longer in the [DefaultUI](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/views/ui/DefaultUI/#components) and is now a part of the View. To control the visibility of the attribution, use the `attributionVisible` property on the [Map](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-map/#attributionVisible), [Scene](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-scene/#attributionVisible), [Link Chart](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-link-chart/#attributionVisible), and [View](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/views/View/#attributionVisible).
- The `attributionText` property on the [Attribution widget](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/widgets/Attribution/#attributionText) is no longer accessible. To access the attribution text, use the `attributionItems` property on the [Map](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-map/#attributionItems), [Scene](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-scene/#attributionItems), [Link Chart](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-link-chart/#attributionItems), and [View](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/views/View/#attributionItems), which contains an array of attribution items with their respective text and score.
- The [SceneLayer.convertMesh](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/layers/SceneLayer/#convertMesh) now returns an object containing the converted `mesh` and optional `georeferenceInfo`. It no longer returns a [Mesh](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/geometry/Mesh/) directly.
- The [projectOperator](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/core/geometry/operators/projectOperator/) may apply a linear Z unit conversion for projecting geometries with Z values (when needed and supported).

### Map components breaking changes [map components breaking changes permalink](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/release-notes/\#map-components-breaking-changes)

- State property changes no longer reflect to DOM attributes. As a result, any CSS or JavaScript that depended on observing state via reflected attributes (for example, attribute selectors, `getAttribute`, or mutation observers) will no longer work and should be updated to rely on property values or other supported APIs.
- The [Time Zone Label](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-time-zone-label/) component is no longer expandable by default. To keep the previous behavior, add Time Zone Label to an [Expand component](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-expand/) explicitly.
- The `expandDirection` and `expanded` properties have been removed from [Time Zone Label](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-time-zone-label/) since it no longer manages its own Expand behavior.
- The [Time Zone Label](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-time-zone-label/) component no longer displays a `time-zone` calcite icon by default. To display an icon, set its [`actionIcon`](https://developers.arcgis.com/javascript/latest/references/map-components/components/arcgis-time-zone-label/#actionicon) property to any [Calcite icon](https://developers.arcgis.com/calcite-design-system/icons/).

The following components, methods, properties and events have been removed from the API:
